description.content: Manage documents within envelopes including adding, retrieving, and deleting documents attached to envelopes.
Retrieves a list of documents associated with the specified envelope. Returns metadata about each document including the document ID, name, type, and order.

variable.description: (Required) The external account number (short) or account ID GUID of the DocuSign account.
(Required) The envelope ID (GUID) of the envelope.

response.name: Successfully retrieved document list.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2
Retrieves the specified document from the envelope. Returns the document content as a PDF or other supported format.

variable.description: (Required) The external account number (short) or account ID GUID of the DocuSign account.
(Required) The envelope ID (GUID) of the envelope.
(Required) The unique identifier of the document within the envelope. Use "combined" to retrieve all documents as a single PDF.

response.name: Successfully retrieved the document.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2
Create, send, and manage envelopes. An envelope is the fundamental unit of a DocuSign transaction, containing the documents to be signed, the recipients who will sign them, and the workflow rules.

Creates and optionally sends an envelope. An envelope is the container for documents, recipients, and the workflow information. You can create an envelope in draft status to allow for further modifications, or send it immediately upon creation.

variable.description: (Required) The external account number (short) or account ID GUID of the DocuSign account.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2
Returns a list of envelopes that match the specified criteria. Use query parameters to filter by date, status, and other envelope properties.

query.description: Start date for the envelope search. Only envelopes created on or after this date are returned. Format is ISO 8601.
End date for the envelope search. Only envelopes created before this date are returned. Format is ISO 8601.
Filter by envelope status. Valid values include created, sent, delivered, signed, completed, declined, voided, deleted.
The status value checked for in the from_date and to_date range. Default is changed.
Maximum number of envelopes to return. Default is 100.
Starting index position for the result set.
Free text search across envelope metadata including recipient names, email addresses, and custom fields.
Sort order for results. Valid values are asc and desc.
The envelope property to sort by. Valid values include created, completed, last_modified, sent, status_changed.
Comma-separated list of folder IDs to search within.
Comma-separated list of additional information to include. Valid values include custom_fields, documents, attachments, extensions, folders, recipients, powerform.

variable.description: (Required) The external account number (short) or account ID GUID of the DocuSign account.

item.name: Docusign Add Recipients to an Envelope

request.name: Docusign Add Recipients to an Envelope

description.content: Adds one or more recipients to an envelope. The envelope must be in draft or sent status. For sent envelopes, this creates a corrected version of the envelope.

query.description: When true, resends the envelope after adding recipients.

variable.description: (Required) The external account number (short) or account ID GUID of the DocuSign account.
(Required) The envelope ID (GUID) of the envelope.

request.name: Docusign List Tabs for a Recipient

description.content: Retrieves the tabs associated with a specific recipient in an envelope. Returns all tab types assigned to the recipient.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2
Create and manage reusable templates that define documents, recipients, tabs, and routing for common agreement workflows.
Retrieves a list of templates associated with the specified account. Templates define reusable envelopes with predefined documents, recipients, tabs, and routing.

query.description: Maximum number of templates to return. Default is 100.
Starting index for the result set.
Search text to filter templates by name.
Folder from which to retrieve templates. Valid values include all, drafts, inbox, sent_items, recyclebin.
Comma-separated list of folder IDs to search within.
When true, returns templates shared by the current user.
Sort order for results. Valid values are asc and desc.
Template property to sort by. Valid values include name, modified, used.
Filter templates used on or after this date.
Filter templates used before this date.
Comma-separated list of additional information to include. Valid values include custom_fields, documents, folders, notifications, recipients.

variable.description: (Required) The external account number (short) or account ID GUID of the DocuSign account.

response.name: Successfully retrieved templates.

The DocuSign eSignature REST API provides a powerful, convenient, and simple web services API for interacting with DocuSign. It enables developers to integrate electronic signing capabilities directly into applications and websites, allowing businesses to send, sign, and manage documents securely and efficiently with support for sequential and parallel signing workflows, document management, templates, and real-time status tracking.

Contact Support:
 Name: DocuSign Developer Center
 Email: devcenter@docusign.com
